---
title: ""
---

```{=html}
<div class="page-header">
  <h1><i class="bi bi-file-earmark-text-fill"></i> Course Syllabus</h1>
  <p>ECO 1000 · Principles of Economics · Fall 2026</p>
</div>
```

## Course Overview

This course introduces students to the fundamental principles of economics. We examine how individuals, firms, and governments make decisions in a world of scarcity, and how those decisions shape markets, prices, and living standards.

**Credits:** 3
**Meeting times:** *[Add your meeting days/times here]*
**Location:** *[Add your classroom here]*

---

## Instructor Information

| | |
|---|---|
| **Instructor** | Prof. Brandon Sheridan |
| **Office** | KoBC 118 |
| **Email** | [bsheridan4@elon.edu](mailto:bsheridan4@elon.edu) |
| **Office Hours** | *[Add your office hours here]* |

---

## Course Objectives

By the end of this course, students will be able to:

1. Apply the core principles of scarcity, trade-offs, and opportunity cost to real-world decisions.
2. Analyze supply and demand and explain how markets reach equilibrium.
3. Evaluate the effects of government policies such as taxes, subsidies, and price controls.
4. Distinguish between microeconomic and macroeconomic perspectives.
5. Interpret basic economic data and connect course concepts to current events.

---

## Required Materials

- **Textbook:** *[Add textbook title and edition]*
- **E-Book / Online Platform:** See the [Learning Resources](resources.qmd) page for access instructions.
- No additional materials to purchase — all other readings will be provided on this site.

---

## Grading

| Component | Weight |
|---|---|
| *[Assignment category 1]* | *[X]%* |
| *[Assignment category 2]* | *[X]%* |
| *[Assignment category 3]* | *[X]%* |
| *[Exams / Quizzes]* | *[X]%* |
| **Total** | **100%** |

Grading scale: A (93–100), A- (90–92), B+ (87–89), B (83–86), B- (80–82), C+ (77–79), C (73–76), C- (70–72), D (60–69), F (below 60).

---

## Course Policies

### Attendance
Regular attendance is expected. Economics builds on itself — missing class makes it harder to keep up. Please notify me in advance if you must miss a session.

### Late Work
*[Add your late work policy here.]*

### Academic Integrity
All work you submit must be your own. Violations of Elon's Honor Code will be reported. See also the [AI in this Course](ai.qmd) page for guidance on using AI tools.

### Accessibility
If you have a documented disability and need accommodations, please contact [Disabilities Resources](https://www.elon.edu/u/academics/koenigsberger-learning-center/disabilities-resources/) and reach out to me early in the semester.

---

## Course Schedule

*[Add your week-by-week schedule below, or link to a separate schedule document.]*

| Week | Topic | Readings |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | Introduction: What is Economics? | Ch. 1 |
| 2 | Thinking Like an Economist | Ch. 2 |
| 3 | Supply and Demand | Ch. 3–4 |
| … | … | … |
